restaurant_openings.name: Levitation Announces Sleep as Secret Sunday Headliner
restaurant_openings.summary: Talk about the Sunday Scaries. Levitation has announced that foundational stoner/doom metal band Sleep will close out the psych rock-and-beyond music festival, performing September 13 at Radio/East. Reunited sludge metal quartet Kylesa, new-gen shoegazers Her New Knife, and locally launched power trio the Well have also joined the event lineup. Kylesa plays their first Austin […] The post Levitation Announces Sleep as Secret Sunday Headliner appeared first on The Austin Chronicle .

restaurant_openings.name: United States Makes Early World Cup Statement
restaurant_openings.summary: The first week of the 2026 World Cup delivered just about everything you could want from a tournament’s opening stanza. FIFA’s “supersize me” World Cup has already treated us hypnotized viewers with a gluttonous feast of stunning results, star-making (and star-affirming) performances, and enough narrative threads to weave a small blanket. events_calendar.name: Spend your summer evenings at the LBJ Library.
events_calendar.summary: Family Spend your summer evenings at the LBJ Library. From May 27 through September 2, the LBJ Library will stay open until 9 PM every Wednesday (last visitor admitted at 8 PM), giving you extra time to explore history after hours. events_calendar.name: The Muppet Movie
events_calendar.summary: Stock up on milk and ice cream ahead of this capsaicin celebration as your very own Chronicle rounds up the best and spiciest sauces to tantalize your tastebuds. Your ears get in on the tingling action, too, as fire live music makes this the hottest happenin’ in town. The Muppet Movie Tue., June 23 Flix Brewhouse Round Rock

The web’s more dangerous than ever, with social-media megafreaks trying to suck up your data and AI dorks begging for your GPU power like the dogs they are. Maybe it’s time we went back to a better era, when the worst you had to fear was Jonny Lee Miller hacking your mainframe as Zero Cool. There is no worse itch to scratch than ambiguity. In his breakout second feature, director Bong Joon-ho refused to give a salve to his home nation of South Korea with this fictional crime drama based on the real-life and then-unsolved Hwaseong Murders, in which 15 women were murdered and an unknown number assaulted. Adapting Kim Kwang-lim’s 1996 play, Come to See Me, it’s a relentless exploration

Austin movie lovers’ favorite rite of summer – over 50 years running – will look a little different this year once the Paramount’s long-awaited renovation project kicks off mid-June. Never fear: They’ll still be screening classic films, only at other venues, like the State Theatre and Hyperreal Film Club. 512/474-1221 Diana Greenberg: “Expanding Line” Through June 28

We’re flush with showcases for many formats here in artsy Austin, but in this latest group exhibition from Women & Their Work, the focus is on just one: drawing. Yes, every artist displayed in this show kicking off on Saturday, May 16, offers their take on the line-based technique from traditional to experimental. “Each piece,” writes W&TW, “exemplifies drawing as both practice and possibility.” F

Everything, for the most part, is bigger in Texas – and that includes the community celebrations. Kicking off this Saturday, April 25, and running until late September is the Bob Bullock Museum’s tribute to the festivals of the Lone Star State. One of the most deeply felt and honest portrayals of the transgender experience comes from the fantastical bildungsroman where young Lucille must travel from Girl Island to Boy Island through a treacherous sea.
